name: First Federal Savings and Loan Association of Valdosta
description_short: FDIC-insured savings and loan association in Valdosta, GA offering personal and business deposit accounts, lending products, and digital banking services.
description_long: First Federal Savings and Loan Association of Valdosta is a regional financial institution based in Valdosta, Georgia, with 91 locations serving the local community. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit security for customers. They operate under NMLS #761813 and offer traditional savings and loan services to both personal and business customers in their market area. The company provides a comprehensive range of deposit products including checking accounts, money market accounts, savings accounts, and CDs. For borrowers, they offer personal loans, residential mortgages, residential construction loans, real estate investment property financing, home equity lines of credit, second mortgages, and business lines of credit. Their digital services include online banking, mobile banking, mobile deposit, online bill pay, Zelle transfers, and eStatements, all accessible through their Fiserv Solutions platform. First Federal distinguishes itself as a community-focused institution with multiple physical branch locations (91 listed) and a primary contact number (229.244.0164) suggesting established local relationships. They offer specialized services like safe deposit boxes, night depository, card valet protection, and their proprietary "My Card Creation" debit card personalization feature. The institution emphasizes security and convenience through various digital channels and traditional banking services. As a regional savings and loan, First Federal caters primarily to customers seeking relationship banking with local branch access rather than national online banking convenience. Their service offerings are standard for traditional institutions, with no indication of premium rates, specialized lending programs, or unique competitive advantages. The website provides basic information but lacks detail on interest rates, loan terms, or fee structures, requiring direct contact for specific product pricing.
services: Online bill pay and Zelle transfers
Safe deposit boxes and night depository
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing of U.S. Government
91 physical branch locations providing extensive local access and in-person service
Comprehensive digital services including mobile banking, mobile deposit, and online bill pay
Personal lending options including mortgages, construction loans, and home equity lines of credit
Business lending products including business checking and business lines of credit
Safe deposit boxes and night depository services for document and cash security
cons: Limited geographic presence as regional institution compared to national banks
Website lacks displayed interest rates, APY information, or fee schedules accessible without contact
No indication of premium savings rates, rewards programs, or competitive product differentiation
Business lending limited to checking accounts and lines of credit with no mention of SBA loans or specialized business services
No digital account opening process mentioned; requires branch visit or phone application
best_for: Valdosta and surrounding Georgia area residents seeking local branch banking relationships
Small business owners in the region needing community bank lending and deposit services
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits and physical branch access over online-only banking
Borrowers seeking traditional mortgage and home equity lending from an established local institution
diagnosis: First Federal Savings and Loan is best suited for Valdosta-area customers who value local branch relationships and FDIC-insured deposits from an established regional institution. The main caveat is that interest rates and fee structures are not transparently displayed online, and the institution lacks the digital convenience and competitive rates typically offered by national or online-first banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: First Federal Savings and Loan Association of Valdosta
